mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides automatic scaling, updates, and integrated monitoring, making it suitable for production workloads. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This command installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and runs the installation script.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace 'your_compartment_id' and 'your_vcn_id' with your actual OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file kubeconfig --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.
commands: oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file kubeconfig --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.
commands: oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command cleans up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id ocid1.loadbalancer.oc1..your_load_balancer_id --force

```

> Replace 'your_load_balancer_id' with your actual load balancer OCID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version 1.30.2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.
> Verify that the nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the kubeconfig file is not correctly set up or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the kubeconfig file is correctly configured and the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Check if you have deployed any applications or switch to the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the user does not have the necessary permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your user has the appropriate IAM policies assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the VCN or subnets are not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Verify that the VCN and subnets are correctly set up and that the necessary IAM policies are in place.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved load balancer configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from a version below 1.30, ensure that your applications are compatible with the new features.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file kubeconfig --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools installed and configured before proceeding.
